title: Adaptive Scanning

content.question: What external signal are you missing that could affect your next 12 months?
sop: Set a fixed weekly time to scan industry news, competitor moves, and adjacent trends.
Extract one specific insight per scan, not just 'stayed informed,' an actual takeaway.
Rate your Awareness and Responsiveness, 1-10 each (see calculation, page 4), how much you actually see, and how fast you act on what you see.
Test one small tweak based on that insight within the week, not next quarter.
Log what you scanned and what changed as a result, so scanning doesn't become passive reading.
questions: What external signal are you missing that could affect your next 12 months?
When did you last change a decision because of something happening outside your business, not inside it?
If a competitor or shift in your industry blindsided you this month, would it actually be a surprise, or something you'd been ignoring?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10: Awareness (how much of the relevant external landscape you actually track), Responsiveness (how fast you act on what you find). Awareness 7 times Responsiveness 5 equals 35. Higher means you catch shifts early and adjust quickly; lower means you're either not watching, or watching and not acting.
content.closing_statement: Watching without acting is just information collection.

caption:
Principle Nº 203: Adaptive Scanning

Monitor outside trends.

Ask yourself: What external signal are you missing that could affect your next 12 months?
